headline: Government lobbying remains completely unregulated in Malaysia
context: Malaysia has no lobbying register, disclosure requirement, or regulatory framework governing interactions between lobbyists and government decision-makers.

cards.big: Zero lobbying regulations — any corporation can influence policy with no public record
cards.sub: Malaysia has no register, no disclosure rules, and no transparency for lobbying

cards.big: No legislation defines, regulates, or requires disclosure of lobbying activities
cards.sub: Entire profession operates in legal grey zone without public accountability

cards.big: Top 10 Malaysian conglomerates spent estimated RM450 million on government relations in 2025
cards.sub: Influence spending happens at scale with no public visibility or oversight

cards.big: EU Transparency Register covers 12,500 organisations with mandatory disclosure rules
cards.sub: Mature democracies regulate lobbying because unregulated influence breeds corruption

cards.big: If lobbying happens in the dark, citizens can never know whose interests their government actually serves
Should Malaysia establish a mandatory lobbying register with public disclosure requirements?
